one_line_thesis: Midwest regulated utility compounding rate base via $12.4B 2026–2029 capex and data-center load; at $75.70 the base return is 5.47% per year — quality franchise, not cheap enough to accumulate.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Alliant Energy is a Midwest regulated utility compounding rate base through a large renewables, storage, and gas build-out while paying a growing dividend. FY2025 earnings per share reached $3.15 on $4.36 billion of revenue; Q1 2026 earnings per share of $0.87 rose 5% year over year. At $75.70 per share (Yahoo close, July 9, 2026), Marvin's base case implies about 5.47% annual return over seven years on starting owner cash of $3.15 per share, well below our ~15% bar. The regulated franchise supports a narrow moat and partial asymmetric payoff (regulated floor, but interest-rate and executio...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Net income moved +17.4% versus the prior period (690.0 to 810.0).
Basic EPS moved +17.1% versus the prior period (2.7 to 3.1).
Board targets 2026 annual dividend $2.14 per share (+5%); quarterly rate $0.535 beginning February 2026
FY2025 net income $810M (+17% YoY); diluted EPS $3.15; revenues $4.36B; long-term debt $10.95B
Iowa Utilities Commission approved IPL retail electric rate settlement including base-rate moratorium through September 2029
Executed at-the-market equity distribution agreement (May 2025) to fund portion of construction plan
Published 2026-2029 construction plan: ~$12.4B focused on renewables, storage, dispatchable gas, and distribution for data-center and load growth
WPL received proceeds from neighboring utilities exercising West Riverside ownership options (partial sale)
Continued execution of Clean Energy Blueprint: coal retirements and renewable additions across Iowa and Wisconsin jurisdictions
Accelerated wind and solar additions; expanded energy storage pipeline to meet MISO capacity requirements
West Riverside combined-cycle gas plant entered service in Wisconsin; cornerstone of dispatchable generation strategy
Wisconsin rate cases incorporated renewable cost recovery and earnings-sharing mechanisms
Leadership team aligned on net-zero carbon goal by 2050 and interim 2030 emissions reduction targets

industry.structure: Regulated electric and gas utilities in Iowa and Wisconsin operate as state-granted monopolies with returns set by the Iowa Utilities Commission and Public Service Commission of Wisconsin; Alliant Energy parent holds IPL and WPL as primary subsidiaries.
industry.share_shift: Midwest investor-owned utilities are adding renewables and dispatchable gas to serve industrial and data-center load growth; Alliant is among utilities bidding for large-load interconnections while managing rate-moratorium constraints in Iowa.
industry.trend: Federal and state clean-energy mandates, MISO capacity margins, and data-center electricity demand are driving record capital spending; higher interest rates and tariff risk on solar equipment are current headwinds.

*Primary risk: Capital spending overruns or regulatory disallowance delay rate-base recovery while $10.95 billion of debt magnifies interest expense.
If data-center load connects faster than planned, Wisconsin and Iowa earnings could beat moratorium constraints.
